__fixture__.name: open_without_restore_session_declined
__fixture__.scenario: User opens the workspace without restoring previously open buffers, a pinned terminal session, or the last remote attach. The session restore store is read intact but the auto-reopen action is skipped this launch.

title: Open without restore after user decline
summary: User opened the workspace without session auto-reopen. Only the auto-reopen action is skipped; the session restore store itself is untouched, so relaunching with restore reenters the prior posture.
preconditions: The user expressed explicit_user_choice at launch or declined session restore after a repeated_startup_failure signal.
The session restore store is readable and intact; this rung does not enter when cache_integrity_failure or policy_blocked is observed.

linkage_bindings.notes: A bundle is usually not produced from this rung; if one is, current_rung_class = open_without_restore.
Doctor stays in observe_only baseline while this rung is active; no write is proposed.
Escalation handoffs cite this rung only when the user needs auto-reopen but the rung keeps blocking it.

explanation_fields.preserved_work_summary: Authored files, durable workspace indexes, trust, credentials, managed policy, and the session restore store itself all remain untouched.
explanation_fields.disabled_capability_summary: Session restore auto-reopen, remote helper attach, and background rebuild are skipped for the current launch. Extensions still load normally once the workspace is open.
explanation_fields.escalation_summary: Escalate when the user needs auto-reopen but the rung keeps blocking it, when exact_undo is no longer available, or when a required capability stays disabled across multiple launches.
explanation_fields.user_facing_next_step: Reopen the tabs the user needs manually, or relaunch with session restore enabled. No export is required for the default path.
